channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 183 replies — 681.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 183 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:41 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62457193
>>62457184
ultimately it's not going to benefit BBBY losers or GME owners.

it's meaningless noise signifying nothing except that the BBBY BK is winding ever closer to its finish without shareholders getting a dime.

62457200
>>62457193
that's because the bathroom gods will be getting way more than a dime

62457215
>>62457200
He asked for an honest answer and I gave it

you are getting nothing

if you get something from a class action lawsuit (unlikely) it's probably going to be less than a hundred dollars total. But in reality there's a long line of actual creditors and lawyers in front of you that will take any payments that might be won.

62457221
>>62457215
we won though

62457223
>>62457221
>we won though
retard of the year award perhaps.

62457418
>>62457215
less than a hundred per share aint bad tho.

I wonder what RC & Icahn are up too?

62457938
>>62449186
What is the legal process to uncancel a stock and reopen short positions that were closed upon cancelation? I can't seem to find it in current US securities law? AI can't find it for me either and is also unable to find me any cases of this ever happening. Looks to me like this is not possible under current law.

62457968
>>62457938
It's the exact same as if you were naked short a stock that issues a dividend. The company doesn't issue more money, they shorts simply are on the hook to pay for it.
Naked shorting creates synthetic longs. Disbursements to the shareholders by the estate/corporate entity are calculated by the actual TSO. It is not the prerogative of the company to "honor" counterfeits. The shorts pay for it.

62458028
>>62457938
what your looking for is the fact that the chapter 11 plan can still legally change, and equity/cash can be distributed to previous shareholders.

then what this poster said:
>>62457968

Short positions aren't reopened because they were never closed since the company is still in active chapter 11, and when the company give $ and shares to each previous shareholder they only have to make distributions based on the number of shares issued, not the extra billions that were created by market makers as Bed Bath pushed under 10 cents.

Nice try paid stock basher, but nothing you say can argue with reason.

62458266
>>62457938
There is no plan to “uncancel” the stock. That is a strawman argument you are forced to beat into the ground.

62458308
>>62457184
GAMEshire BATHaway
>t. pulte
>t. sultan

62458310
>>62458028
>Nice try paid stock basher
I think this whole thing must be some elaborate comedy bit, or you people are just insane. Good luck though.

62458385
>>62457184
>>62458308
It's as shrimple as that

62458481
>>62458308
>>62458385
Will this lead to MOASS?

62458756
>>62458481
We will finally be free

62458765
>>62458756
Don't say stuff that makes me feel sorry for you

62459593
>>62458765
Nothing really to feel sorry for other than some lost time. We already know the shareholder interests have been preserved because we know the CUSIP is still being used. We're just waiting to see what truly took place and from there we'll get our massive tendy payout.

62459685
Imagine missing out on the single greatest victory for retail investors in the entire history of finance. Not me because I am a bathroom god.

62459707
>>62459685
It really hasn't set in yet that we're getting extremely close to the finish line here. Normally I am kind of sad about the summer days passing by. But this fall is going to be spectacular.

62459753
>>62459707
Tendie status: in the fryer
Butterfly status: emerging from the cocoon
Shill status: in shambles

62459815
>>62459753
It's all coming together nicely. And it's not even just a small little payout either. We have billionaires calling this thing Gameshire Bathaway.
It's pretty awesome being on the inside of this joke being made. That goes for all of us, Maxipads included.

62459836
>we call GME maxi pads because they are all being used to soak up the fertilizer from $GABBBYQ's aunt flo reverse swaps at the holiday inn

62459881
0€ in my account now.
From 10.000€ in 2023 and 10k share BBBYQ.

62459892
>>62458756

62459901
I'm m very disappointed.
Europoor meanwhile.

62460453
>>62459836
>we
You seem to have an issue with conflating one posters mean words for who you’re forced to pretend to be with everyone that notices your disingenuous nature.

62460901
>>62437938
I just realized Larry Cheng wasn't even there, he is on a fucking monitor kek

62461047
>>62460901
He still cared enough to tune into that niche live show that happened on some random night outside of business hours.

62461136
Jake2SNEED is hosting a space on Twitter at noon on July 10th.

62461170
>>62461136
holy fucking shit bathroom gods are FEASTING

item.title: >go into trades >be HVAC or plumber >Make 100k by year 3 Its that easy huh? — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>go into trades >be HVAC or plumber >Make 100k by year 3 Its that easy huh?

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:55 UTC

OP

>go into trades
>be HVAC or plumber
>Make 100k by year 3

Its that easy huh?

Early replies (first 3)

62452524
>>62452507
No. Lol

62452597
>>62452507
This is the marketing tagline for trade schools. You'll note, like all marketing campaigns, there's alot they're not telling you.

62454749
>>62452507
nope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62454991
The real secret trade is aircraft maintenance. I get paid over $100K a year to sit around and wait for shit to break as a line technician. Reactive, not proactive maintenance. I bought a steam deck just to occupy my time when I’m not doing shit. Only downside is the night shift, but I’ve grown to enjoy it. I’m hoping I’ll get dayshift in a few years.

62455009
>>62454991
>in a few years
Bro has relegated himself to zombie status for years just for a measly 100k lmao. Night shift in your youth is fucking laughable

62455040
There's a oversupply in trades. People are having a hard time getting an apprenticeship. Why would not every illegal just get into plumbing if it's so easy and good money.

62455180
Lmao just work 60 hours a week year round goy.

62455189
>>62455040
You have to have a license to be a plumber, don't you? Illegals can't get licensed. Also the reason why Electrician and HVAC are still the /white man's trades/

62455203
>>62455189
When did you have a plumber last time. The world has changed my friend

62455249
>>62452507
It really is that easy. You can even start your own business within a few years of getting enough field experience and make even more money if that’s your thing. I would personally rather just not deal with that and be a wagie. I make enough money as it is and I don’t have to worry about taking the work home with me.

62455286
>>62455040
There is no oversupply. I will never have a hard time finding a job. hvacr work isn’t limited to just comfort cooling. There’s a demand for apprentices too because retention rate seems to be low, at least around where I’m at. Training apprentices takes time and the demand to fill in the roles of people retiring is there

62455334
>>62452507
I make more than that in year 2 and don't go through poop water and asbestos

62455349
>>62455189
Most of those plumbers are old white men now hiring the illegals. It's different state to state but here, as long as someone on site has a license(the old white man owner), everyone else is just a sub contractor not required to be licensed because the owners insurance covers them.

62455422
>>62454991
Did you go straight to getting your A&P, or did you join the military? I was thinking about joining the national guard to do rotarcraft maintenance. I would just go to A&P school, but I want to ride in them too. I figured crewchief experience would look good to wildland firefighting agencies or those transportation companies that do offshore shuttling.

62455429
>>62452507
>have BS & MS in mechanical engineering
>year 5 into HVAC engineering
>making 90k + incentive/performance bonus
>TC of $120-140k depending on year
>be the guy that signs the paycheck for trades people
Unless you own the company you aren't making over 100k in the trades.
That's a meme.
Most of the guys I pay are making at most $60k (medium CoL area), with tons of overtime they *might* make $75k. Which is still better than some non-STEM degree holder that has $100k in student loans.

"Go learn a trade" is the new "learn to code" and we all saw how that worked out. The moment people all flood into a singular field, that field dies for decades.

62455895
>>62455429
>Unless you own the company you aren't making over 100k in the trades.
>Most of the guys I pay are making at most $60k (medium CoL area), with tons of overtime they *might* make $75k
Our scale down here is $38 on the check (total benefits package ~$57) and we are the lowest in the state, probably also on the list of one of the lowest in the country even. You would only need to average around 47 hours a week to clear $100k. We have guys that willingly pull in crazy overtime and easily clear $100k. The other locals in the state make mid to upper 40s. Remember, that’s just the scale. That isn’t counting techs who make above that, which most techs do

62455955
>>62452507
>yeah my dad's boyfriend's wife's son's step dog's cousin makes $700k doing underwater HVAC brazing on the international space station only works 3 days every other leap year btfo collage fags

62455982
>>62452507
>>62454991
>tell your position and wage to the whole world so that everyone else will try doing the same and will end up flooding the market and lowering wages

62456079
>>62452507
Father started his HVAC business three years ago. Here in Prague, he went from $28k/year (when he was an employee) to around $250k/year (currently) and he keeps getting new customers and projects. He even got the official government inspector stamps for multiple HVAC fields, so he has nice passive income just from revisions.

62456093
>>62456079
I bet you guys are busier now because of the heat waves. Here in the US HVAC techs make the most money in the summer fixing and installing AC units

62456175
>>62456093
True, I think that is applicable globally. I don't work with him though, I have my own small IT company. But my father is now turning down many jobs and potential clients, and only works for the best paying customers, if even that. He always lived a frugal life and never much cared for wealth and that hasn't changed, so besides spending some money on his closest ones to improve their life, he simply has no need for more money, so every week he has situations like this
>phone rings, hotel owner on the line
>"Mr. Anonsfather, the wagies are being cooked, the guests are redeeming their bloody stays, please, come at once"
>"My apologies Mr. Shekelstein I simply don't have the time. The soonest I can get there is in three weeks"
>*click*
>sips on boomer lemonade, sitting in his garden, moisturized, in his lane, flourishing

So yeah, I think having your HVAC business might be nice

62456826
>>62455189
>he thinks people need a loicense to connect 2 pipes

62456856
>>62452507
you have to work in dangerous environments and around chemicals and toxic boomers. You will have several near death experiences doing any trade. You have to work around the scum of society. That's what they don't tell you

62456872
>>62454991
That's so you can afford a lawyer if you fuck it up.

62457420
>>62456856
>you have to work in dangerous environments and around chemicals and toxic boomers. You will have several near death experiences doing any trade. You have to work around the scum of society. That's what they don't tell you

62457477
>>62452507
no, the people who make $100k either manage the business or own it.

trades suck as an employee.

62457583
>>62455982
I don’t think the whole world is looking at this 4chan thread

There’s a good chance this post will convince nobody to actually become an aircraft technician. Half of us are NEETs after all

62457587
>>62452507
clean foids poop

62457660
>>62457587
I do that for free

62457707
>>62457587
>>62457477
White incel loserthink

62457871
>>62455955
>tfw 40 years old and making a bit less than the median for my occupation (not plumber)
Feels bad man.

62461107
one of my friends dropped out of hs at 15 to become a plumber

he eventually got hos master plumber license went into biz for himself and now is swimming in money

62461220
>>62452507
there's a path to that salary, but if you don't you can just take all the copper wire and pipes your company technically owns.
personally, I noticed a lot of old timer installers who havn't kept up with refrigerant oils don't install properly, so it breaks down after a year and I get all the slightly corroded copper.

item.title: MUH SHINY ROCKS!!! — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
MUH SHINY ROCKS!!!

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:53 UTC

OP

WHAT THE FUCK?

Early replies (first 3)

62460369
Mmmmmmhh.....
Shiny rocks are in his head....
Mmmmmmhh.....
The rocks dumped when he slept....

Somewhere out there's a nigga
Lying to himself....
"Hyperinflation is coming"
This nigga is in hell

62460445
>>62460369
song?

62460559
>>62460445
inspired by "again - xxxtentacion"

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460618
>>62460315
>I sold my silver stack at almost twice the current price
>silver spergs will STILL think I did the wrong thing
told ya so

62460634
>>62460618
I sold too nigga i am not that fucking greedy, but i only sold half.

62460642
I asked /pmg/ why it never rallies and they gave me a spiel about being patient and holding for 20 years like a real man

i invested in miners.

62460649
>>62460634
that's probably the best choice
I should probably restart a collection starting pretty soon

62460678
>>62460649
Prices are looking good now IMO

62460776
silver is currently at 3x the amount I paid for it,,,,,,why are you guys freaking out,,,,,lmao

62460782
>>62460776
>4 decades for a measly 3x

62460783
>>62460642
As usual when it comes to PMs, it's about interest rate expectations. Before Trump nominated Warsh and before the Iran war, expectations for interest rates ran a lot lower

62460926
You're not supposed to buy PMs to make fiat $. You make fiat then buy PMs to exit the fiat system.

62460988
>>62460926
Ok faggot now put the 1980 - 2000 gold chart.
Fucking jackass

62460996
>>62460618
That's not cool dude some first time trader from New Delhi bought that bag off of you and now he's probably homeless. Give the money back.

62461118
>>62460988
Keep kvetching
I'll keep being a bad goy and buy more silver on this dip

62461119
>>62460782
lmao,,,,,cope more loser,,,,,

62461132
>>62460642
>invested in minors

62461183
>>62460926
we've been there for 10 or 15 years now

62461209
China isn't stockpiling gold because they plan to make a bunch of pretty jewelry. Figure out why and plan accordingly. And take into account the probability of the US government reigning in its spending orgy and debt accumulation.

item.title: Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed. — 36 replies — 73.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:27 UTC

OP

Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Now many crypto's like XRP and Chainlink are no longer illegal and the banks can start buying it.

Are you ready for the pump of a lifetime in the coming weeks?

Early replies (first 3)

62444894
I just took 70mg of illicit amphetamine sulfate

62444895
>>62444894
That's very nice to hear, I hope you are enjoying yourself.

62444904
>>62444895
it's nothing like meth (oral ROA meth had no euphoria either), minimal vasoconstriction; none really. not euphoric like adderall either, it feels like super non-diuretic caffeine. this has a 1:1 dextro:racemic ratio; so the pharma equivalent is evekeo.

I thought it was bunk but my heart rate is elevated but no arrhythmia like meth (that shit is poison, it'll keep you up for 3-5 days with no perceptual fatigue at significant cost)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444929
>>62444922
i do not

62444934
>>62444929
alright, I appreciate it regardless. thoughts on CBDCs?

62444939
>>62444934

I actually dont know much about that also

62444940
>>62444939
you sound like a nice guy no homo

62444948
these 2 got married y'know

62444959
>>62444940
Haha thanks man you too

>>62444948
Nooo we are just friends haha

62444978
>>62444892
> CLARITY ACT
wasn't there some announcement announced for yesterday?

62444991
op they haven't voted on it and aren't close to voting on it. they're hoping to get it done before august 10

62444995
>>62444991
Somone told me clarity act was 4th of july a few weeks ago

62445015
>>62444948
naw, the no homo made marriage impossible
>>62444959
>we are just friends
this

62445147
>>62444978
No, Trump did an interview restating that the US will be the crypto capital of the world.
>>62444892
CLARITY will pass, but there is a lot going behind the scenes that's causing it to stall. Did you know that CLARITY allows banks to buy and sell crypto? The last time US banks traded stocks was before the great depression.

62445160
>>62445147
Oh that's very interesting, thanks for sharing my friend

62445194
So, two more weeks?

62445212
>>62444948
you're responding to a samefag trying to keep his thread at the top because no one cares.

>>62444892
no one cares

62445217
>>62445212
that's not me

62445568
Why isnt crypto pumping harder?

62448360
>>62444892
The banks can already buy both. ETF inflows broke records despite being from small issuers on XRP, highest of any asset on the year, while inflows are borderline non-existent for link.

62448734
Everyone itt thread is a retarded bot.
Fake thread.

62450765
>>62444892
BOOOOOOOOOM

62450968
I hate niggers

62451473
>>62450968
Kaboom

62454381
>>62444892
>banks start buying it
okay jeet

62454867
>XRP

So. Fucking. Bullish.

62458344
>>62444892
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62458384
>>62444892
>this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.
nope

62458627
>>62444995
that date was always nonsense because they are literally in recess from the end of june until july 13. they will have another month to pass it before they go into recess yet again in august

62459378
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62460039
>>62444978
2 more weeks turned into 1 more month

62461072
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62461114
>>62460039
It's not going to pass.

Republicans don't have enough votes to force it through congress and in a few months republicans are getting kicked out of congress.

item.title: my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc… — 47 replies — 52.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:03 UTC

OP

my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a successful entrepreneur and just completely failed. he's flat broke now. it's crazy to me how hard it is to make good money even if you're smart and hardworking. the world is fucking brutal.

Early replies (first 3)

62449875
>>62449873
“Smart” and didn’t go all in crypto. I shiggy diggy

62449876
Almost like being a business owner is just being a wagie with more work and all of your capital at risk.

62449881
>>62449876
Nah that's being self employed, an entrepreneur convertes other peoples time and labour into money

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450101
>>62449873
I got paid a full day at my job on Thursday for LITERALLY doing nothing but writing my magnum opus novel, watching my niece and playing pokemon showdown.


It's a skill issue. Not an IQ one.

62450119
>>62449876

If you work “in” the business it’s just like owning your job.

62450121
>>62449942

Tata motors which owns Jaguar and Land Rover.

62450123
>>62450101

You have a job tho, it’s different than trying to start a business

62450129
>>62449887
top silicon valley ceos are all high iq, top wallstreet executives too, there's just no way you can survive that kind of environment without constantly observing obscure patterns, so your shallow analysis doesn't track
>>62449873
your brother in law probably failed because he wasn't part of the group, he probably tried to muscle into someone else's turf, what business was he into?

62450177
>>62450121
I know of someone who own a land rover, a newer model. He went for a drive in sand with it and the engine burned out, he got it replaced and went down to the beach to drive over sand dunes. Engine burned out again

Lmao aryan quality, I didn't know tata acquired land rover in 2008 but it makes sense now

62450181
>>62450077
You took your vaccines like a good cattle everyone knows you poojoos are the most subservient minions

62450429
>>62450077
Castes aren't a real thing it's some turdjeet larp among themselves.
In reality there's only turdjeets and actual human (white) people.

62450441
>>62450429
Face it you white incel losers are the lowest caste people in the world. Unlike Indians NO ONE likes you or wants you around AND you are BROKE

62450451
>>62450441
Get off that white dick poopoid

62450460
>>62450451
Post some more about how you are broke and depressed but deserve an easy life because you are white

62450481
>>62450460
I deserve an easy life and I have an easy life while you're broke and depressed and looking for some dick

62450485
>>62449873
I'm retarded and have made millions.
Just the way it goes sometimes OP.
Being smart isn't a ticket to success crazy enough.

62450500
>>62449873
BTDT. I was lucky that Bitcoin came along when it did or I'd be living in a cardboard box sucking off meth addicts.

62450584
>>62449873
>150+ IQ
>worked his ass off over the last 15 years
158 IQ here. Only one of those statements can be true (probably the 2nd). If he were actually that smart he'd have known how to optimize things so he didn't need to work his ass off. I've been loafing about the last few decades and my liquid net worth is just over $4m having barely lifted a finger.

62450615
>>62450584

This is where we mention there is autistic "intelligent", and then there is intuition. Some people can solve anything you put in front of them but can't inductively synthesize a shrewd or creative idea. AI brain, basically lol.

62450621
>>62449887
High IQ overthink, underact, avoid risk taking
>AAAHH! I have tooo thiiiiiink first
Low IQ underthing, overact and dont even register risk

62450636
>>62450584
Wanna invest in my super secret club?

62450659
Naybe OP isn't.smart enough to recognize whether somebody os a genius. What if the brother in law is actually a dumb ass and has people fooled?

62451127
>>62449873
These bots think they are posting their demoralization threads on reddit now

62452004
>>62449873
making it is more about emotional regulation than about iq past like 110 or so

62454704
>>62449873
BORING

62454725
>>62449873
Business is literally only about
>distribution
and
>managing skills of others
Doesn't even matter if yo ruin someone's wedding or house or roof or anything by hiring an imbecile
You got hired
and if you can get hired at volume
that's the hardest fucking part
getting users on your platform, getting people to give you their money
there are plenty of Mexicans and shit willing to work
the hard part is getting the work
You can have the best idea in the world, be a better carpenter than Jesus' dad,
but if you're invisible, the dude who has signs all over town with an IQ of 88 and does a shit job will get filthy fucking rich.

62459089
I'm 150+ IQ and I can say for sure that being smart is not what made me successful. I was lucky enough to learn the right skills and apply them in the right environment.

With money, it's not about how much you know. It's about knowing the right thing.

62459128
>>62459089
so you were lucky to know the right thing faster than anyone else in your vicinity but this has nothing to do with iq, you don't seem very smart

62459153
>>62459128
Well in addition to earning money via skills I also invested money in an intelligent way so as to multiply that income. That for sure wasn't something a dumb person could've done without aid.
When it comes to getting skilled in an area with strong demand and good pay though - it's actually better to be dumb. "Smart" people routinely waste decades of their lives becoming qualified in unemployable fields. Dumb people just get the trade qualification and live their best life.

By the way, IQ testing specifically measures shape-rotation, mathematical skills and logic. Not the most lucrative traits, unless correctly applied.

62459170
>>62459153
lmao, you sound like a midwit

62459175
>>62459170
Congrats on being the 587113rd person to feel threatened by me, I guess

62459190
>>62459175
well done for feeling scared enough to defend your unlucrative trait that is iq

62461019
>>62449873
I'm OP's in-law. I'm trans btw and top OP

item.title: AI is dying — 37 replies — 31.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI is dying

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-07-08 18:47 UTC

OP

>Over the weekend, I got a message from a tech company CEO outlining his TOTAL BAN ON AI across his organization. There are no exclusions. It is not limited to generative AI. It is not temporary. It is a full stop.
>It turns out that the corporate dream of a single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

Sorry not sorry, you've been warned multiple times but kept throwing money into casino chips aka AI stocks.

https://www.inc.com/joe-procopio/the-first-company-wide-ai-ban-just-hit-my-inbox-heres-what-it-means/91357535

Early replies (first 3)

62449971
>The Ban on AI Research
>The CEO’s company had been dealing with a slump in conversion rates for the better part of 2026
>Turns out, AI-assisted research had resulted in casting a much wider net for prospects and dragged in many that were not nearly a fit. In some cases, the companies were defunct, and in one case, the company didn’t exist at all. The best the CEO could figure is that AI had just inferred it into existence

62449974
>The Ban on AI Agents
>One of the agents broke, in that was unable to scrape data it needed to generate a report. Its solution was to just generate the report anyway and then to freak out about the sudden change in what that report was saying, now that it was missing foundational information but treating it as if that entire business line had suddenly gone to zero.
>“It took a week to figure it out, because the guy who created the agent wasn’t the guy responsible for the report, and he was stalling while re-running months’ worth of reports by hand.”

62449977
>The Ban on Vibe Coding
>The reporting failure was the trigger for the ban.
>“When I discovered we had two different versions of the same module in our platform, connected to two different databases filled with two different sets of data stored in two completely different and unrelated [schemas], the next day I put the ban in place.”

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450135
>>62450012
Speaking for myself, I do not like the idea of poors entering my country club.

62450162
>>62449963
It isn't dying, but people are waking up to the fact that it's not "Jarvis, run my company" situation and that there is a hard skill curve to coordinating agent pipelines.

62450194
>>62450012
True, but the game is already rigged for the casino either way.

62450337
Not reading all that but AI isn't going anywhere, the pace may slow down but it's not going to be binned off.

62450351
CEOniggers once again proven to be 40 IQ retards.

62450414
>>62449963
Our CEO is going all-in on AI for software development and it has boosted our throughput by 2-3x. It's not slop code, either.
We have unlimited tokens.
t. SWE at startup

62450423
>>62449963
Over the weekend my ceo told me we are adopting more ai

62452074
>>62450414
>It's not slop code
lol. LMAO even

62452087
>inc.com
What
>a tech ceo
Which company

62452090
>>62450012
>rich people investing 3 trillion into AI the last 4 years
>Nvidia a 5 trillion dollar company
>the AI market is over now, because it's over
Makes you think.

62452160
>>62450135
Exactly. There is the door so would you kindly leave?

62452209
Kek my company banned it years ago when some high level executive fed sensitive financial data to chat gpt. It qas back when chat gpt was new and novel and he was probably a boomer that didn't know what chatbots are and thought it was a super intelligence.

62452880
AI is a technology and it won't go way, it will evolve into different tools that will enhance the production. The problem is that corporations are using as an excuse to control the resources and make big data centers to get more info from you and sell it.

62452896
>>62449963
This article is a month old, fucktard.

62452909
>>62452896
Precisely, baggie.

62452925
>>62452909
continuation pattern

62452935
>>62452925
lmao

62452942
>>62452935
>you: heh my pattern is bearish
>"i disagree, I think it's bullish"
>REEEEEEEE TA SUCKS FUCK YOU

62453140
>>62452942
I never implied that. I implied you're delusional if you believe exponential growth is any sustainable long-term.

62453190
>>62453140
Probably because you are retarded.

62454009
>>62452074
All the code is written and reviewed by hand. We have custom AI agents for repetitive tasks and custom tool calls error-checking.
Works great, amazing ROI

62454042
>>62449963

There is no way I am going to ever write a regular expression or a complex SQL statement without AI EVER AGAIN.
AI solution saves time and is easy to verify.

62455423
>>62449963
its joever

62455925
>>62454009
We don’t even review our AI code anymore. Our CTO admitted the pace at which we are vibe coding quality code and feature delivery far outweighs the cons and our SWEs can keep up with helping us troubleshoot but can’t keep up with actually reviewing the code. We launched a new product and between me and one other person built the whole thing over 2,000 PRs from scratch in 3 weeks. Currently customers on it generating 6 figures in new revenue monthly, almost zero issues and when they do happen 98% of the time I just take the error throw it at Claude and it’s fixed 10m later. Crazy. I have zero coding experience btw, though I was a product manager for many years so led multiple teams of SWEs and have a clear advantage over most non coders in using AI for that and many other reasons. I keep thinking I should sell my house because I realize I’m pretty early to understanding how truly powerful this shit is and I think a lot of people are going to lose their jobs within the next few years, though I hope I’m wrong.

62460197
>>62455925
source: your ass

62460285
>>62449963
i guess i can play along with this
we bring AI underground, where it should have always been, and away from luddite normies

62460438
AI IS USED NOWHERE BUT A MODICUM OF TECH SECTOR

>The first chart below shows that so far there are no signs of profit margins rising outside the tech sector. This is ultimately what we are waiting for, because the value of AI companies today rests entirely on the promise that margins in the S&P 493 will eventually climb.

https://www.apollo.com/wealth/insights-news/insights/daily-spark/ai-the-roi-runway-could-be-long-outside-the-tech-sector

62460476
>>62452090
To be fair they just get gov bailouts if it all goes to shit so it's literally a 0 risk game for them

62460488
>>62460197
People like you are honestly so funny to me. What good does remaining ignorant do you, really? Find out the hard way, I guess. You will remember the stories like mine when you are fully replaced because nobody wants to employ a dumb twat they have no use for at all. The game hasn’t even started yet, most companies have no idea. They will all shift in time.

62460961
>>62455925
>>62450414
What type of software? Is it new stuff, or is it another case of reinventing the wheel with customer-specific tweaks for the billionth time and mostly plagiarizing existing open source code while skirting the licensing?

item.title: Crypto/blockchain is finally, factually, concretely moving towards the integration we've been dreaming of (well not as c… — 6 replies — 21.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto/blockchain is finally, factually, concretely moving towards the integration we've been dreaming of (well not as c…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:40 UTC

OP

Crypto/blockchain is finally, factually, concretely moving towards the integration we've been dreaming of (well not as cypher-punk as many of us have hoped and still hope for), yet the total crypto marketcap is not acting like it. Swift and the DTCC are getting on board, the BIS wrote a whole chapter about crypto in their annual economic report, we have the clarity act coming, etc. etc. Has AI just taken that large of a piece of the capital pie? I just don't get why more institutions are not piling into crypto right now. Are we waiting for pension funds, endowments, etc to be able to hold more than BTC/ETH? There's been a decent amount of price appreciation for select altcoins over the last year or so, but their mcaps are still far below previous tops. I guess those previous tops were often too feverish though...

Early replies (first 3)

62460957
>>62460903
1K EOY

62460963
just don't respect stinks
simple as, bitch

62461064
>>62460903
the tech is being integrated into the legacy financial system
note that the tech is more than the tokens
normies will trade tokenized equities 24/7 in the future but it will be abstracted from them that they're tokenized
your tokens days of making good money for holders are over, at least until prediction markets get regulated into the grave and uncle sugar locks everyone up and gives them free money again

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461087
>>62460903
>Swift
>DTCC
stinkie spotted, nobody cares about your single digit shitcoin btw

62461133
>>62460903

There is a key difference between Adoption and Assimilation. Institutions using blockchain? Yes absolutely. Using your XRP or whatever fag token? Nope

BTW AI took the narrative(+dollar) and any here still talking crypto is a dumb fucking laggard. Myself included.

>>62460957
>>62460963
>>62461087

Stupid XRP army faggots, you are already dead and you don't know it. BTW if there is one token that actually gets a slice of the pie, It's LINK but my indication is they let us all die a quiet ignominious death.

>>62461064

That's an optimistic view at best.

62461166
Imagine still talking about tech and utility in the year 2026. lol token not needed. It never was it never will be.

item.title: How sensitive are the markets? — 7 replies — 19.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
How sensitive are the markets?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:35 UTC

OP

If you buy lets say $10M of NVDA or TSLA on spot, would you get dumped on immediately?

Early replies (first 3)

62459526
>>62459506
If you bought on margin then yes, the exchanges work for the men in little hats and they will try to liquidate you.

62459538
Yes. Most things will be someone's exit pump. A few things went up yesterday other than oil though. Maybe you should pay attention to those.

62459563
>>62459526
This!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62459858
>>62459506
>>62459526
So the key is to trigger a dump and then buy that dip? Assuming someone had more than 10 mil.

62459918
>>62459506
No, such thing does not exists on spot. Only maybe cost of opportunity, but if everything is going down it doesn't matter.

62459964
>>62459506
Depends on trading volume. As a rule, for a purchase of that size, you'd want to break it down into smaller orders so you don't get raped by high-frequency traders. Or just use an over the counter service.

62461145
>>62459858
Yes, you can say you will nuke Iran to have a perfect entry.

item.title: Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years. — 11 replies — 13.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-08 19:07 UTC

OP

Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years.

Most people think they’ll sell XRP and simply buy back lower.

They still don’t understand what happens when real utility creates real demand, real economic activity, and real liquidity. That’s a completely different market.

As more organizations connect to the network, demand doesn’t just appear… it compounds. That’s how multi-trillion dollar markets are built.

Humanity has never witnessed a utility driven financial transformation on this scale. For the first time, we get to live through it together.

Excited for the next chapter.

Early replies (first 3)

62450170
It's had more than a decade for all that, ain't happening champ

62450645
>>62450165
Xrp was $3.20 in 2018 lol, shut up faggot

62450808
>>62450170
>>62450645
Obtuse gay jewish fingers typed these.

So bullish

62453970
>>62450808

62453976
>>62450165

How can you not see the wisdom of owning a coin that is down over 50% in 6 months.. everyone that is selling is a fool... BUY more... PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE buy more... I have to sell before the rent is due!!!

62454861
So fucking bullish XRP

62456256
>>62450165
